title: phanera involucellata
intro: Phanera involucellata, also known as the "giraffe flower," is a climbing plant native to the tropical forests of Asia, characterized by its unique, large, and showy flowers. The medicinal properties of Phanera involucellata include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic. In traditional medicine, Phanera involucellata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and treat various ailments.
